response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with broad experience across many languages and platforms, but she is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple critical dimensions. The most disqualifying gap is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, her background is almost entirely web, mobile, and application-layer software; she has no demonstrated experience in embedded networking firmware, L2/L3 security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel development, or switch hardware — all of which are core to this position. Her C exposure appears minimal and dated.
strengths: Long career spanning 15+ years with consistent delivery across diverse tech stacks, demonstrating adaptability and engineering maturity
Some embedded and low-level systems exposure (NI LabWindows C/C++ work, hardware control at Altom, Sound Position Server with Linux/Jack/C++)
Strong English proficiency satisfying the documentation and email communication requirement
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated hard requirement and is an immediate disqualifier
No experience with networking security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC auth) or L2/L3 switch architecture
C programming is not a demonstrated core skill; Linux kernel investigation and source-level modification experience is absent
